etymology.description: 日(日) (rì, "sun") + 寺(寺) (sì, "temple") as phonetic = time marker influenced by the sun's movement -> 寸(寸) (cùn, "inch") added in associated compound
etymology.interpretation: The character 時(shí, "time") combines the semantic element 日(日) (rì, "sun") with the phonetic element 寺(寺) (sì, "temple"). The concept of measuring time relates to the sun's movement; 寸(寸) (cùn, "inch") later added emphasized the notion of time as a measurable entity.
etymology.simplification: The simplified character 时 is a cursive handwritten variant of 時, streamlined by omitting the 寸(寸) component.

examples.chinese: 时间就是金钱(時間就是金錢)。
每当我想你时(每當我想你時)，心情就会变好(心情就會變好)。
examples.pinyin: Měidāng wǒ xiǎng nǐ shí, xīnqíng jiù huì biàn hǎo.
examples.english: Whenever I think of you, my mood improves.

examples.chinese: 雨季来临时(雨季來臨時)，我们需要准备好雨具(我們需要準備好雨具)。
examples.pinyin: Yǔjì láilín shí, wǒmen xūyào zhǔnbèi hǎo yǔjù.
examples.english: When the rainy season arrives, we need to prepare our rain gear.
